title: adonis ambigua subsp. flava
intro: Adonis ambigua subsp. flava, also known as Adonis flava, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. It belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive leaf shape. The medicinal properties of Adonis ambigua subsp. flava include its potential to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditional herbal preparations of Adonis ambigua subsp. flava include infusions and decoctions, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and promoting digestive health.
